summary.intent: Evolve Gossamer from a single-repo hardcoded setup to a multi-repo architecture where each repo has its own SQLite database, a shared config file at ~/.gossamer/config.json, and a UI for onboarding and monitoring multiple repos.
summary.outcome: Multi-repo architecture fully implemented: config file management, per-repo SQLite DBs with schema migration on onboard, polling indexers covering all repos, Repos onboarding UI, repos status view with sessions/repos toggle, BranchLog screen showing all checkpoints on a git branch, and CheckpointDetail view matching SessionDetail's expanded layout.

repo: Each repo gets its own SQLite db; config lives at ~/.gossamer/config.json with fields: name, remote, localPath, dbPath.
The server's primary db is at ~/.claude/hook-handler.db — onboarding the gossamer repo itself must reuse this path, not generate a new empty one.
Checkpoint IDs are embedded in git commit bodies as 'Entire-Checkpoint: <id>' trailers; CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps OIDs to checkpoint IDs for older commits that predate the trailer format.
git branch --show-current returns empty string on detached HEAD; symbolic-ref --short HEAD is the correct fallback.
The localPath in config had a symlink mismatch (/Users/stsoucas vs /Users/stephanostsoucas); serve.ts now uses realpathSync before comparing paths.
prisma db push --skip-generate is idempotent and safe to call on already-migrated databases.

code.finding: repoForCheckpoint() searches primary db first then all configured repo dbs to resolve cross-repo checkpoint lookups.
/api/branch-log now uses git log --format=%H%x1f%B%x1e with ASCII delimiters to parse Entire-Checkpoint trailers, supplemented by CheckpointIdGitOidJoin, with db WHERE branch= as final fallback.
ensureWorktree must be inside try/catch in indexCheckpointsForRepo — mkdirSync with EACCES would crash the whole server if uncaught.
React synthetic events are pooled/nulled by the time a functional setState updater runs — capture e.currentTarget.value synchronously before passing to setForm(f => ...).
POST /api/repos dbPath resolution order: existing entry's dbPath → server's primary dbPath if localPath===repoDir → defaultDbPath(name). This prevents orphaning existing data on re-onboard.

workflow: Use prisma db push --skip-generate (not migrate dev) for schema application on new per-repo dbs — avoids regenerating the client which is shared.
Fire indexers with void after onboarding so the HTTP response returns immediately while worktree setup and initial indexing run in background.
ASCII unit/record separators (%x1f, %x1e) in git log --format are the correct way to delimit multi-line commit bodies without ambiguity.
When multiple repos share a server, reuse existing open PrismaClient connections (matched by dbPath) to avoid opening stale/empty db files.

friction: Config had wrong localPath and dbPath for gossamer repo (symlink path + empty db instead of real hook-handler.db) — caused hours of 'no data' for gossamer in repos view.
CheckpointDetail was stripped too aggressively after removing the missing /api/checkpoints/:id/messages route, leaving users with a near-empty view.

git log → CheckpointIdGitOidJoin approach for BranchLog only returned one checkpoint because those OIDs are from entire/checkpoints/v1 branch commits, not working branch commits — required two rewrites.
The /api/v2/checkpoints/:id 'Not found' issue was caused by cross-repo checkpoint lookups, not a route typo — misleading error message.
Mantine TextInput onChange synthetic event nulling caused a runtime crash that required understanding React event pooling to diagnose.
open_items: BranchLog fallback (WHERE branch= db query) is only used when git finds nothing — if branch is deleted or has no trailers and the db is stale, data may be incomplete.
The /api/repos/status latestCheckpointId still comes only from the db — no git-based fallback for repos whose db hasn't been indexed yet.
CheckpointDetail diff view uses fetchCheckpointDiff which internally uses repoForCheckpoint — if the checkpoint is in a non-primary repo, the repoDir for git diff must also be resolved correctly.
No UI feedback during onboarding while prisma db push and initial indexing run in background — user has no way to know when indexing completes.
The gossamer.db at ~/.gossamer/repos/gossamer.db is now orphaned (empty schema, no data) — should be cleaned up or the config tooling should warn about unused db files.
